---
title: "4.6 Using Text Files"
subtitle: "Data Collections"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Files let programs use external data

Text files store data outside the Java program. A program can read a file, parse values, and save them into arrays or objects.

Core classes:

::: {.tight-list}
- `File`
- `Scanner`
- `IOException`
:::

## File and Scanner

### Open a file for reading

```java
import java.io.File;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.util.Scanner;

public class ReadFile
{
    public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException
    {
        File file = new File("data.txt");
        Scanner input = new Scanner(file);
    }
}
```

`throws IOException` acknowledges that file input can fail.

## Reading Values

### Scanner reads tokens or lines

```java
while (input.hasNextLine())
{
    String line = input.nextLine();
    System.out.println(line);
}
```

Common scanner methods:

::: {.tight-list}
- `hasNextLine()`
- `nextLine()`
- `hasNextInt()`
- `nextInt()`
:::

## Loop to Read a File

### File reading is usually a loop

```java
int count = 0;
while (input.hasNextLine())
{
    String line = input.nextLine();
    count++;
}
System.out.println(count);
```

The loop continues until there is no more file input.

## Save File Data into an Array

### Store data for later processing

```java
String[] names = new String[100];
int count = 0;

while (input.hasNextLine() && count < names.length)
{
    names[count] = input.nextLine();
    count++;
}
```

The `count` variable records how many array slots were actually filled.

## Split Strings

### CSV data becomes fields

```java
String line = "Pikachu,35,Electric";
String[] parts = line.split(",");

String name = parts[0];
int speed = Integer.parseInt(parts[1]);
String type = parts[2];
```

`split` returns an array of substrings.

## Object-Oriented Design with CSV

### Convert rows into objects

```java
public class Pokemon
{
    private String name;
    private int speed;

    public Pokemon(String name, int speed)
    {
        this.name = name;
        this.speed = speed;
    }
}
```

Each row can become one object after parsing.

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Array of Pokemon from input file

Starter task:

::: {.tight-list}
- read each line from a Pokemon file
- split the line by commas
- parse numeric fields
- create a `Pokemon` object
- store it in an array
:::

Class focus: file input, arrays, parsing, and class design working together.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- open a text file using `File` and `Scanner`
- explain why file-reading code may throw `IOException`
- use a loop with `hasNextLine`
- store file data into an array with a count variable
- split a CSV line and parse numeric fields
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
